2605.18656.title: Statistical Limits and Efficient Algorithms for Differentially Private Federated Learning
2605.18656.paper_summary: Federated Learning is a leading framework for training ML and AI models collaboratively across numerous user devices or databases. We study the trade-offs among estimation accuracy, privacy constraints, and communication cost for differentially private (DP) federated M estimation. The two standard methods in the literature are FedAvg, which may suffer from high federation bias, and FedSGD, which can incur high communication cost. Aimed at improving accuracy at a reduced communication cost, we propose FedHybrid, which uses FedSGD starting with an improved initialization by the FedAvg estimator. We propose FedNewton, which averages local Newton iterations to reduce bias in FedAvg, achieving an estimation accuracy comparable to FedSGD with much fewer communication rounds when the number of clients grows sufficiently slowly. We establish finite sample upper bounds on the mean-squared error rates of the DP versions of these estimators as functions of the number of clients, local sample sizes, privacy budget, and number of iterations. We further derive a minimax lower bound on the MSE of any iterative private federated procedure that provides a benchmark to assess the optimality gap of these methods. We numerically evaluate our methods for training a logistic regression and a neural network on the computer vision datasets MNIST and CIFAR-10.
2605.18656.paper_summary_zh: 聯邦學習是一個領先的框架，用於在眾多用戶設備或數據庫之間協作訓練機器學習和人工智慧模型。<lb/>我們研究了在不同ially private (DP) 聯邦 M 估計中，估計準確性、隱私限制和通信成本之間的權衡。<lb/>文獻中的兩種標準方法是 FedAvg，可能會遭受高聯邦偏差，以及 FedSGD，可能會產生高通信成本。<lb/>為了在降低通信成本的同時提高準確性，我們提出了 FedHybrid，該方法使用 FedSGD，並以 FedAvg 估計器的改進初始化開始。<lb/>我們提出了 FedNewton，該方法通過平均本地牛頓迭代來減少 FedAvg 的偏差，當客戶數量增長足夠緩慢時，實現了與 FedSGD 相當的估計準確性，且通信回合數大幅減少。<lb/>我們建立了這些估計器的 DP 版本的均方誤差率的有限樣本上界，作為客戶數量、本地樣本大小、隱私預算和迭代次數的函數。<lb/>我們進一步推導出任何迭代私有聯邦程序的均方誤差的最小最大下界，為評估這些方法的最優性差距提供了基準。<lb/>我們對在計算機視覺數據集 MNIST 和 CIFAR-10 上訓練邏輯回歸和神經網絡的方法進行了數值評估。
2605.18656.author: Arnab Auddy et.al.
2605.18656.authors: Arnab Auddy, Xiangni Peng, Subhadeep Paul
